content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 02. Data Model
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md
Document section: 02. Data Model / 3. Главные сущности / 3.5 `Claim` .. 3.6 `Profile`
Document lines: 311-417

### 3.5 `Claim`

Атомарный факт или relation edge.

Это ключевая таблица для:
- точных factual answers
- predicate-based reconciliation
- explainable retrieval

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `claim_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id`
- `subject_entity_id: uuid`
- `predicate: text`
- `object_entity_id: uuid | null`
- `object_value_json: jsonb | null`
- `normalized_text: text`
- `context_text: text | null`
- `scope_json: jsonb`
- `qualifiers_json: jsonb`
- `confidence: numeric(4,3)`
- `importance: smallint`
- `status: active | superseded | retracted | expired | candidate`
- `retrieval_state: active | warm | cold | archived | deleted`
- `valid_from: timestamptz | null`
- `valid_to: timestamptz | null`
- `origin_note_id: uuid | null`
- `first_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `last_seen_at: timestamptz`
- `supersedes_claim_id: uuid | null`
- `retracted_by_claim_id: uuid | null`
- `created_from_run_id: uuid | null`
- `created_at: timestamptz`
- `updated_at: timestamptz`
- `embedding: vector | null`
- `use_count: integer`
- `last_used_at: timestamptz | null`
- `last_validated_at: timestamptz | null`
- `archived_at: timestamptz | null`

Отдельная таблица `claim_sources`:
- `claim_id`
- `source_id`
- `support_type: direct | inferred | imported | derived_from_note`
- `evidence_span`
- `evidence_quote`
- `support_confidence`

Инварианты:
- один claim = одно predicate application
- claim должен иметь смысл без исходного чата
- explicit statement > inference
- relative time должен быть нормализован
- если смысл еще слишком мягкий, лучше `Note`, чем плохой `Claim`

---

### 3.6 `Profile`

Компактная always-in-context проекция.

Это не source of truth, а projection layer поверх:
- explicit user instructions
- stable claims
- selected long-lived context

Рекомендуемые поля:
- `profile_id: uuid`
- `namespace_id`
- `owner_entity_id: uuid`
- `profile_json: jsonb`
- `profile_text: text`
- `version: bigint`
- `updated_from_run_id: uuid | null`
- `last_compacted_at: timestamptz | null`
- `created_at`
- `updated_at`

Рекомендуемая структура:

```json
{
  "preferred_name": null,
  "languages": [],
  "timezone": null,
  "response_preferences": {
    "response_language": null,
    "tone": null,
    "detail_level": null,
    "code_comment_language": null
  },
  "expertise": [],
  "stable_preferences": [],
  "constraints": [],
  "current_goals": [],
  "soft_signals": []
}
```

Инварианты:
- profile должен быть коротким
- explicit facts важнее inference
- спорные facts лучше оставлять в claim/note, а не тащить в profile
- profile должен быть пересобираем

---

        Candidate notes:
        [0] type=DOC_DIGEST title=`Claim` schema role and quality invariants in v3 summary=In `02. Data Model` section 3.5, `Claim` is the atomic fact/relation unit for precise factual answers, predicate-based reconciliation, and explainable retrieval; each claim should represent one predicate application and remain self-contained, with explicit statements preferred over inference and weak/soft meaning redirected to `Note` instead of low-quality claims. scope=Applies to section 3.5 `Claim` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<0>,entity:<1> keywords=Claim,predicate-based reconciliation,claim_sources,invariants tags=data_model,v3,claim,schema_invariants evidence=Инварианты: - один claim = одно predicate application - claim должен иметь смысл без исходного чата - explicit statement > inference - relative time должен быть нормализован - если смысл еще слишком мягкий, лучше `Note`,...[truncated 19 chars]
[1] type=DOC_DIGEST title=`Profile` as compact rebuildable projection layer summary=Section 3.6 defines `Profile` as a short, always-in-context projection (not source of truth) built from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context; it should prioritize explicit facts over inference, keep disputed facts in `Claim`/`Note`, and remain rebuildable. scope=Applies to section 3.6 `Profile` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md entities=entity:<2>,entity:<1> keywords=Profile,projection layer,always-in-context,rebuildable tags=data_model,v3,profile,invariants evidence=Это не source of truth, а projection layer поверх: - explicit user instructions - stable claims - selected long-lived context

        Existing active notes:
        id=hot-path:note:<0> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=v3 data model uses layered memory architecture with two cross-cutting concerns summary=In `v3`, the data model is intentionally split into layers (`Source`, `Entity`/`Claim`, `Note`, `Profile`/`Task`/`Episode`, `MemoryRun`) to support typed memory, lineage, temporal truth, reconcilable updates, richer semantic context, lifecycle/retention, and repairability; `scope` and `retrieval lifecycle` are defined as cross-cutting concerns. scope=Applies to section '02. Data Model / 1. Общая идея .. 2. Общие cross-cutting поля' in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=v3 data model,layered memory architecture,scope,retrieval lifecycle tags=data_model,architecture,memory_layers,cross_cutting useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<1> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Source` component role and invariants in v3 memory model summary=`Source` is the raw evidence layer that stores chat/tool/imported/external records and supports re-check and explainability. It should be immutable in practice, keep `observed_at` separate from ingestion time, use `content_hash` for dedupe, and enforce explicit delete policy. scope=Applies to section 3.1 `Source` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Source,evidence layer,observed_at,content_hash,delete policy tags=data_model,memory_architecture,invariants useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<2> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`PredicateCatalog` defines reconciliation behavior per predicate summary=In the v3 memory model, `PredicateCatalog` is a configuration component (not a memory item) that controls how each predicate is reconciled, including cardinality, temporal semantics, conflict handling, and whether facts project into profile/task layers. scope=Applies to section 3.3 `PredicateCatalog` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=PredicateCatalog,reconciliation,cardinality,temporal_policy,conflict_policy,profile_sync,task_sync tags=data_model,memory_v3,schema_policy useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Note` is an intermediate semantic memory layer with lifecycle and linkage summary=`Note` is explicitly positioned between raw sources and hard claims: a single self-contained semantic fragment for retrieval-worthy context (decisions, rationale, plans, hypotheses, lessons, digests) with status/retrieval/maturity lifecycle, evidence linkage, entity linkage, and note-to-note relations. scope=Applies to section 3.4 `Note` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Note,semantic fragment,retrieval_state,maturity_state,note_entities,note_sources,note_links tags=data_model,memory_v3,note_design useCount=0
id=hot-path:note:<4> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=`Entity` as canonical memory anchor with reversible merge policy summary=`Entity` is the canonical memory node used to unify mentions and anchor claims/tasks/notes/episodes, with alias and merge support. Merge must preserve history, aliases must resolve to the current canonical entity, and incorrect merges must be reversible via audit trail. scope=Applies to section 3.2 `Entity` in agent_memory_handoff/02_data_model.md keywords=Entity,canonical node,entity_aliases,merge,audit trail tags=data_model,entity_resolution,invariants useCount=0

responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.
